fragment:
<set-variable name="corsOrigin" value="@{
        var origin = context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("Origin", "");
        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(origin)) { return ""; }

        var allowedOriginsJson = context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("allowedOriginsJson", "[]");
        var allowedOrigins = JArray.Parse(allowedOriginsJson);

        if (allowedOrigins.Any(o => o.ToString() == "*")) { return origin; }
        if (allowedOrigins.Any(o => o.ToString() == origin)) { return origin; }

        return "";
    }" />
    
        
            
                <when condition="@(!string.IsNullOrEmpty(context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("corsOrigin", "")))">
                    
                        
                        
                            @((string)context.Variables["corsOrigin"])
                        
                        
                            GET, POST, OPTIONS
                        
                        
                            Content-Type, Authorization, X-Requested-With
                        
                        
                            86400
                        
                    
                
                
                    
                        CORS origin rejected by per-API Named Value mapping.
                        
                        
                        
                    
                    
                        
                        
                            application/json
                        
                        {"error": "CORS origin not allowed for this API"}
